title: Port Graph
description: TypeScript surface for **Port Graph** on `SpecdDataPort`, mirroring the HTTP routes in the paired `api:routes-*` group. UI hooks call these methods; adapters implement them over HTTP or IPC.
context: TypeScript surface for **Port Graph** on `SpecdDataPort`, mirroring the HTTP routes in the paired `api:routes-*` group. UI hooks call these methods; adapters implement them over HTTP or IPC.

rules:
The interface MUST declare asynchronous methods equivalent to the HTTP routes in the matching `api:routes-*` group:

- `getGraphStatus()`, `indexGraph()`, `searchGraph(query)`, `getImpact(query)`, `getHotspots()`, spec/change graph views

`indexGraph({ force? })` MUST mirror `POST /v1/graph/index` and return the indexing summary DTO rather than a graph-status DTO.

`searchGraph(query)` MUST expose the full graph-search query surface used by Studio:

- `q`
- `workspace`
- `kinds`
- `filePattern`
- `excludePaths`
- `excludeWorkspaces`
- `symbols`
- `specs`
- `limit`

`getImpact(query)` MUST forward `depth` as part of the graph impact selector.

`getImpact(query)` MUST support `symbol`, `file`, or `spec` as the graph impact selector.

rules.requirement: port signatures are identical for HTTP and IPC adapters
rules: Implementations (`adapter-remote-specd-data`, desktop IPC) MUST implement these methods without altering parameter or return types.

rules.requirement: port failures surface as typed client errors
rules: HTTP failures MUST be translated by `adapter-problem-json-errors` into errors the UI hooks can display.

constraints: @specd/ui and @specd/client MUST NOT import @specd/core or @specd/sdk for kernel bootstrap.
HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact — not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.

scenarios.name: Port methods mirror routes contract
then: each method maps to documented /v1 route
AND types match DTO specs

scenarios.name: indexGraph returns index summary
then: remote adapter POSTs that force flag to /v1/graph/index
AND the returned data shape is the graph-index result DTO

scenarios.name: searchGraph forwards rich graph filters
when: searchGraph(query) includes kinds, filePattern, or exclusion filters
then: the adapter forwards those query fields to /v1/graph/search
AND the returned data shape preserves snippets and line ranges

scenarios.name: getImpact forwards traversal depth
then: the adapter forwards that value to /v1/graph/impact
AND the returned DTO preserves aggregate impact metrics

scenarios.name: getImpact forwards spec selectors
then: the adapter forwards that selector to /v1/graph/impact
AND the returned DTO preserves affected spec ids

scenarios.name: List changes hits collection HTTP route
when: listChanges() is called on remote adapter
then: GET /v1/changes is requested
AND response parses to typed rows

scenarios.name: IPC adapter exposes same method names
when: desktop IPC adapter handles port call
then: method name and arity match interface
AND return shape matches HTTP path

scenarios.requirement: port signatures are identical for HTTP and IPC adapters
scenarios.name: Remote and IPC adapters share types
when: TypeScript compiles both adapters against port interface
then: parameters match
AND return types match

scenarios.requirement: port signatures are identical for HTTP and IPC adapters
scenarios.name: UI hooks do not branch on transport
then: same method signature for HTTP and IPC
AND only adapter selection differs

scenarios.requirement: port signatures are identical for HTTP and IPC adapters
scenarios.name: Signature drift fails build
then: adapters fail compile until updated

scenarios.requirement: port failures surface as typed client errors
scenarios.name: HTTP 404 becomes typed client error
then: adapter throws parseable error
AND hook shows message

scenarios.requirement: port failures surface as typed client errors
scenarios.name: Network failure is not swallowed
then: hook receives error
AND loading state clears

scenarios.requirement: port failures surface as typed client errors
scenarios.name: Success bypasses error mapper
then: no error thrown
AND data returned to hook
